stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 4 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 5 observations with mean 55.2 /10 (within-chain SD = 6.4). chain B has 15 observations with mean 57.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 6.6). chain C has 15 observations with mean 51.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.9). chain D has 15 observations with mean 34.9 /10 (within-chain SD = 9.3). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.5 and 13.6 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain D: 35.8 /10 (shrunk from raw 34.9). Range [35.3, 37.1] as between-chain SD varies from 5.5 to 13.6. Pooled mean: 48.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 48.7 /10 (weighted average of 4 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=9.1: B = 0.935.
Interval [35.3, 37.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (34.9) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (48.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
